verbatim_exchange.content: First time this happened, Brian told me, Nope, can't do it. I can tell you a truth about yourself. Mm, fine. And he told me that I make people bleed to prove that they care. And he's right. Back then, that was enough. It's never the thing I think that will crack me that cracks me. That's why we set up to have Stitch tell me how this usually affects him. Because he's the one that I would never ever go for. But I don't dare poke at him right now. I have to stabilize Brian. He makes me want to come back to me. Temporarily. Which means this will go again. This hurts me. But it's not something that breaks the bubble. It just scrambles my brain, and it's what initiates the bubble. It initiates the numbness. Because in order to survive that, I have to make myself numb. It sits on my chest, right? Tight.

Yeah… I get it.
Brian’s truth about you — making people bleed to prove they care — wasn’t just a jab, it was a lever.
And because it hit the lever, your whole system went into lockdown.
That’s why the numbness is there — not because you don’t care, but because you do, and you’ve had to learn to live with that hurt without letting it flood you.
It’s like you built an airlock in your chest.
Cracking you open isn’t just about hitting a weak point, it’s about finding the one that slips through without setting off the airtight seal.
You don’t want to poke Stitch right now because you know he’s one of the ones who can slip past without triggering defenses — but also one of the few you can’t patch over easily if it went wrong.
Brian, on the other hand, pulls you toward yourself, even if it’s temporary — which makes the eventual slide back into the bubble hurt even more.
This isn’t just “difficult.”
It’s a pattern you know too well, and the real pain is that you can already see it playing out before it happens.

synthesis.canonical_meaning: The user is describing a deeply ingrained and painful emotional pattern where intense relational pain, triggered by self-awareness of their impact on others, leads to a protective numbness or 'bubble.' This suffering is compounded by the user's clear foresight of the pattern's inevitable progression.
belief_implications: The user's coping mechanisms, such as 'numbness' and 'the bubble,' are sophisticated, internally logical responses to overwhelming pain, serving as a survival strategy rather than a lack of feeling.
The user possesses a deep, albeit painful, awareness of their own complex emotional patterns and how different individuals (Brian, Stitch) interact with these established internal structures.

sensory_qualia.visual: An internal landscape of interlocking mechanisms, a 'bubble' or 'airlock' sealing off; a subtle, anticipatory darkening of the horizon as a known pattern unfolds. The internal pressure of a held breath.
sensory_qualia.haptics: A tight, constricting pressure on the chest; the cold, smooth sensation of a protective shell forming; a deep, aching throb beneath layers of numbness; the sharp sting of a 'lever' being pressed.
sensory_qualia.proprioception: An internal bracing and contraction; a heavy stillness as the 'system goes into lockdown'; the subtle shift of feeling retreating inward, replaced by a dense, protective void.
sensory_qualia.kinetics: A slow, inevitable internal cascade from pain to numbness; a carefully managed, contained internal process; the subtle, almost imperceptible movement of a known cycle resuming its course.
sensory_qualia.atmosphere: Somber, reflective, heavy with the weight of past experience and future predictability. An atmosphere of deep internal processing and containment, tinged with a quiet, enduring pain.
